name: CALP Network
role: Global CVA standards & learning network; convenes donors, NGOs and DFS providers on cash programming best practices

name: Anticipatory Action Hub
role: Global learning platform for anticipatory action; connects EAP practitioners, data providers and funders

name: Centre for Disaster Protection
role: UK advisory body on DRF instrument design; FCDO partner; contributed to IFRC-DREF insurance framework

name: Global Shield Solutions Platform
role: G7-backed initiative launched COP27 (2022); coordinates expansion of climate risk insurance and social protection from ~500M to 4B people by 2030; brings together V20 vulnerable countries, G7 donors, insurers and MDBs; politically anchors the premium subsidy rationale central to CCHB; Senegal submitted 2025 Request for Support to fill meso-level protection gaps (MSMEs, cooperatives, agropastoral households)

name: MCII
role: Munich Climate Insurance Initiative; advocates for climate risk insurance as an adaptation tool for vulnerable countries; co-designed the InsuResilience Global Partnership framework; research on parametric insurance, basis risk, and the insurance protection gap in LDCs and SIDS — direct policy reference for CCHB design

name: AFI — Alliance for Financial Inclusion
role: Global network of 100+ central banks and financial regulators from developing and emerging economies; sets inclusive finance policy standards for mobile money and DFS regulation; AFI membership underpins the regulatory environment in CCHB target corridors (Haiti, Vanuatu, Zambia) — key stakeholder for MNO licensing and e-money policy alignment

name: IIED
role: International Institute for Environment and Development; research and policy on climate adaptation, loss and damage, and resilience finance for LDCs and SIDS; influential voice in UNFCCC negotiations on parametric insurance and DRF — generates the evidence base that justifies CCHB premium subsidy mandates

name: LIFE-AR
role: Least Developed Countries Initiative for Effective Adaptation and Resilience; LDC-led platform coordinating climate adaptation priorities across 46 LDCs; advocates for direct LDC access to climate finance including parametric instruments; connects CCHB to LDC political constituency and national adaptation plan processes

name: WRI
role: World Resources Institute; global research on climate resilience, disaster risk, and adaptation finance; produces flagship analysis on climate risk insurance gaps and the economics of anticipatory action — evidence base directly cited in CCHB design rationale

name: CAN
role: Climate Action Network; 1,900+ civil society organisations in 130+ countries; advocacy on loss and damage, adaptation finance and climate justice — shapes the political context within which CCHB donor mandates and L&D Fund are negotiated at UNFCCC COPs

name: USP2030 Climate WG
role: Universal Social Protection 2030 Climate Working Group (ILO/World Bank joint initiative); bridges social protection and climate adaptation — argues for parametric trigger-based cash transfers as climate-adaptive social protection; directly validates the CCHB model within the social protection policy community

name: Swiss SDC/FDFA
role: Swiss development & foreign affairs; premium funder for parametric/cat bond humanitarian instruments; Geneva CCHB anchor bilateral

name: FCDO
role: UK development; co-funded Jamaica cat bond premium; backs Centre for Disaster Protection; key CCHB premium partner

name: Wilsdorf Foundation
role: Geneva private foundation (Rolex family); supports humanitarian innovation in the Geneva ecosystem

name: IMS Foundation
role: Impact-oriented foundation; supports humanitarian and DFS innovation

name: Twilio Impact Fund
role: Tech-for-good fund; SMS/voice/data for nonprofits — relevant for USSD/MNO beneficiary registration in CCHB

name: InsuResilience
role: G7/V20 initiative; funds climate risk insurance for the vulnerable in developing countries; core CCHB premium subsidy mechanism

name: Center for Disaster Philanthropy
role: US strategic philanthropic intermediary; channels disaster-response grants; potential CCHB premium donor

name: White Label Impact
role: Impact investment advisory/platform; structures blended finance for social impact instruments

name: Lloyd's Insurance Incubator
role: Lloyd's Lab accelerator; incubates DRF/parametric startups including Etherisc; product development funding for innovative risk transfer

name: Green Climate Fund (GCF)
role: World's largest climate fund ($15.9B portfolio); IFRC is an accredited entity; approved $103M for EW4All early warning systems (2024); adaptation window directly applicable to CCHB

name: German BMZ / KfW DEG
role: Largest bilateral climate risk insurance funder; InsuResilience co-founder; backs the Natural Disaster Fund (Global Parametrics/Hannover Re); essential CCHB premium subsidy partner

name: Adaptation Fund (AF)
role: UNFCCC adaptation fund; direct access for LDCs/SIDS; faster and smaller than GCF; highly relevant for Vanuatu Pacific CCHB pilot

name: Rockefeller Foundation
role: Backs ARC and parametric insurance pilots; resilience and DRF innovation funder; proof-of-concept grants for insurance-linked humanitarian instruments

name: Bill & Melinda Gates Foundation
role: Backed Pula Series B via IFC/InsuResilience; funds agricultural parametric insurance and DFS financial inclusion at scale; CGAP primary funder

name: CIF-SCF
role: Climate Investment Funds — Strategic Climate Fund; concessional finance accessible only through MDB implementing partners (World Bank Group, IDB, AfDB, EBRD, ADB); targeted programs include PPCR (Pilot Program for Climate Resilience) directly applicable to SIDS and LDC CCHB corridors; requires MDB co-financing structure

name: Conrad N. Hilton Foundation
role: Co-designed WASH blended finance facility with KOIS/UNICEF/Proparco; humanitarian innovation blended structures in fragile contexts — direct structural analogue to CCHB

name: Start Fund / Start Network
role: START Network pooled rapid-response fund and NGO consortium; ARC Replica Partner since 2018 — first non-governmental policyholder in ARC history; purchases drought policies in West and East Africa (Senegal, Somalia, Zimbabwe); disburses within 72 hours of crisis alert; 2019 Senegal drought trigger: six Start Network member agencies (Action Against Hunger, Catholic Relief Services, Oxfam, Plan International, Save the Children, World Vision) distributed enriched flour and made cash transfers to 335,000+ people across seven regions using Replica payout funds — the most complete documented end-to-end parametric humanitarian payout chain yet deployed; directly analogous to CCHB's humanitarian delivery architecture

name: Loss & Damage Fund
role: UN fund for loss and damage from climate change; formally established COP28 (2023); hosted by World Bank transitionally; first capitalisation ~$700M pledged; designed to finance impacts that cannot be adapted to — CCHB sits in the adaptation/response boundary directly relevant to its mandate; potential premium co-funder for SIDS corridors

name: Building Bridges
role: Geneva sustainable finance initiative; annual summit convening ILS/impact/SDG investor community — core CCHB investor outreach and deal-making platform

name: Symbiotics
role: Geneva impact AM; microfinance and blended finance; potential CCHB junior/impact tranche investor

name: 3IF
role: Impact Insurance Innovations Initiative; impact-first ILS/insurance instrument — managed by Rift Partners

name: Convergence Finance
role: Global blended finance network; $231B historical deal database (1,233 transactions); designs and seeds blended structures; key CCHB capital stack matchmaker and convener

name: IFC (World Bank Group)
role: DFI arm of World Bank; first-loss tranche provider; backed Pula Series B; GCF accredited entity; essential for CCHB blended capital stack

name: AFD / Proparco
role: French DFI; blended finance champion; climate adaptation in fragile states; draws on European Blending Facilities and GCF mobilisation

name: EBRD / EIB
role: European development banks; climate adaptation mandates; concessional debt or guarantee layer for CCHB SIDS and fragile-state pilots

name: KOIS
role: Impact finance advisor; designed WASH blended facility with UNICEF/Proparco/Hilton Foundation — a direct structural parallel to CCHB's blended architecture

name: BlueOrchard (Schroders)
role: Impact AM (Schroders subsidiary); led Pula Series B via InsuResilience strategy; climate insurance impact mandates; closest investor archetype to CCHB needs

name: Vision Fund International
role: Impact investment arm of World Vision; invested in IBISA Network; finances inclusive finance and resilience solutions for vulnerable smallholder communities — natural CCHB blended finance partner given shared beneficiary base

name: Acumen Resilient Agriculture Fund
role: Acumen's dedicated fund for agricultural resilience in sub-Saharan Africa and South Asia; patient capital for parametric insurance and climate-smart agri innovations; invested in companies like Pula; concessional first-loss tranche model directly applicable to CCHB capital stack

name: AfDB ABM — Adaptation Benefit Mechanism
role: African Development Bank's Adaptation Benefit Mechanism; certifies and monetises measurable adaptation benefits as tradeable units purchased by donor governments; enables project developers to attract private co-finance by pre-selling certified adaptation results — directly applicable to CCHB payout as a certified adaptation outcome; pioneering results-based climate finance model

name: Asian Development Bank Ventures (ADBV)
role: ADB's innovation and venture arm; backs climate-resilient finance solutions across Asia-Pacific; SIDS and Pacific corridor mandate overlaps directly with CCHB Vanuatu pilot; can provide concessional co-financing or first-loss guarantee alongside the CCHB bond structure

name: Ankur Capital
role: Indian impact VC; invested in IBISA Network; portfolio focused on agriculture, financial inclusion and climate resilience for low-income populations in South Asia; early-stage parametric insurance ecosystem builder

name: AXA Climate
role: AXA's dedicated parametric arm; underwrites emerging market and agricultural parametric products at scale

name: Pula
role: Pioneering insurtech based in Nairobi (founded 2014); uses technology and parametric insurance to reach previously unbanked and uninsured smallholder farmers across Africa and Asia; 21M+ farmers across 19 countries; AI analytics and digital platforms to manage climate risks; $20M Series B led by BlueOrchard/IFC/Gates Foundation; also backed by Acumen Resilient Agriculture Fund

name: IBISA Network
role: Global climate insurtech operating across Europe, Asia, and Africa; partners with leading reinsurers and local insurers to close the protection gap; advanced expertise in parametric insurance design, satellite-based weather monitoring, and automated payout systems; fast, objective, transparent claims with no field assessment required; 700K+ people covered; backed by Vision Fund (World Vision) and Ankur Capital; ClimaCash+ is their flagship humanitarian cash-transfer parametric product — direct CCHB structural analog

name: Lemonade
role: AI-driven digital insurer; instant parametric-like claims processing; tech stack reference architecture for CCHB

name: African Risk Capacity (ARC)
role: Pan-African sovereign parametric pool; subscribed by Senegal since 2014 covering agricultural drought via Africa Risk View satellite index (USD 30M cap); government finances drought policy; PTF co-finance epidemic and livestock policies; ARC Replica mechanism allows CSOs (Start Network, Oxfam) to take mirror policies covering the most vulnerable unreachable by the sovereign mechanism — Oxfam reached 31,942 beneficiaries (16,229 women) in 2023; flood extension under discussion but faces parameterisation challenges; ARC Malawi 2015–16 basis risk is canonical CCHB cautionary tale

name: CCRIF SPC
role: Caribbean & Pacific sovereign parametric pool; covers Haiti, Jamaica and Pacific SIDS — direct CCHB corridor overlap

name: Floodbase
role: Satellite flood monitoring and parametric flood insurance for municipalities; trigger design and underwriting partner for CCHB flood perils

name: Global Parametrics
role: Parametric index design; NDF manager; One Acre Fund Zambia/Malawi parametric payout rails; published Feb 2026 humanitarian cat bond whitepaper

name: Mitiga Solutions
role: Volcano risk modelling; built trigger model for Dunant Re; climate risk analytics for emerging markets and humanitarian contexts

name: AIR Worldwide
role: Industry-standard catastrophe models (Verisk); used in ILS trigger design and SPV documentation globally

name: ParametricsMatters
role: Parametric insurance consultancy; knowledge-sharing on product design, basis risk management and index calibration

name: NASA FIRMS / Lifeline
role: NASA open satellite data (fire, flood, disaster events); provides trigger index data for CCHB parametric instruments at no cost

name: Dataminr Signal
role: Real-time event detection from open data; early warning for trigger events; Dataminr for Good nonprofit access pathway

name: Flowminder
role: Non-profit; mobility and population data from mobile networks; beneficiary mapping and displacement tracking for CCHB trigger zones

name: Google Flood Hub
role: Google Research platform providing AI-powered river flood forecasting up to 7 days ahead for 1800+ rivers across 80+ countries; uses ML models trained on historical streamflow and weather data to generate inundation maps and flood extent predictions at sub-daily resolution; free public API and dashboard — directly relevant as a trigger data source for parametric flood instruments in CCHB target corridors (Sub-Saharan Africa

name: Bright Data
role: Web data platform; structured data collection for trigger index verification and calibration

name: Kettle
role: AI wildfire parametric MGA; clear-cut loss definition methodology — instructive for CCHB trigger index design

name: Replexus Limited
role: ILS securitisation facilitator; ILSBlox blockchain settlement platform; structured and settled Dunant Re via Replexus ICC Guernsey; saves $200–400K per issuance vs traditional settlement

name: Etherisc
role: Decentralised insurance protocol (Generic Insurance Framework); smart contract parametric triggers; Lloyd's Lab alumnus

name: Nexus Mutual
role: Decentralised mutual on Ethereum; risk-sharing pools; potential DeFi capital layer for CCHB surplus absorption

name: onRe Finance
role: Blockchain reinsurance marketplace; tokenised risk transfer instruments

name: InsurAce
role: Multi-chain DeFi insurance; liquidity mining for risk capital; on-chain CVA insurance layer

name: Ensuro
role: On-chain reinsurance capital; stablecoin collateral; smart contract payout automation for parametric triggers

name: Encointer
role: Community currency blockchain (Nyota, Tanzania); CVA multiplier effect and resilience token concept for CCHB beneficiaries

name: Plenum Investments
role: Swiss ILS specialist; cornerstone Dunant Re investor; leads humanitarian ILS impact allocation in Geneva market

name: Schroder Investment Mgmt
role: Global AM; ILS arm invested in Dunant Re; growing ILS and ESG/impact allocation across mandates

name: Solidum Partners
role: Swiss ILS specialist; Dunant Re investor; co-origin of Replexus ILSBlox blockchain settlement platform

name: Pictet
role: Geneva private bank; ILS investment desk; broad sustainability mandate; CCHB ILS investor target

name: Lombard Odier
role: Geneva private bank; participated in blockchain-settled ILS secondary trade; active ILS allocation and sustainability focus

name: Rift Partners
role: ILS-focused investment manager; specialist in collateralised reinsurance; manages the 3IF (Impact Insurance Innovations Initiative) fund

name: Man Group
role: Global AM; published research on cat bonds as impact fixed income (2024); growing ILS/sustainability mandate — CCHB institutional investor target

name: Swiss Re
role: Global reinsurer; ILS structuring; sovereign parametric covers; World Bank partner; essential CCHB risk capacity provider

name: Munich Re
role: Global reinsurer; 1000+ parametric requests/year via Munich Re Capital Partners; routinely structures CCHB-type instruments

name: Hannover Re
role: Global reinsurer; backs One Acre Fund Natural Disaster Fund; co-issued Cumulus Re parametric cyber cat bond (2024)

name: Howden Group Holdings
role: Co-brought Dunant Re to market via Howden Capital Markets; key ILS structuring and placing agent; Howden Foundation co-funded Dunant Re development

name: Aon
role: Global broker; manages Dunant Re IC Ltd (Guernsey) via Aon Insurance Managers; IFRC-DREF insurance partner; CCHB deal structurer

name: Miller
role: Specialist London insurance broker; growing ILS and parametric placement capability; relevant for CCHB humanitarian bond structuring

name: WTW — Climate & Resilience Hub
role: Willis Towers Watson's dedicated climate risk unit; develops Climate and Development Insurance (CDI) — parametric and index-based covers designed for climate-vulnerable developing countries; advisory on sovereign risk finance and disaster risk transfer; direct structural overlap with CCHB premium and trigger design

name: Digicel Haiti (MonCash)
role: Primary CCHB Haiti last-mile rail; MonCash born out of 2011 earthquake humanitarian disbursements; 2M+ active wallets; government social cash transfer platform

name: Digicel Pacific (MyCash)
role: Pacific last-mile rail; Vanuatu recommended pilot; MyCash disaster donation platform activated December 2024; Telstra-owned

name: Airtel Africa
role: CCHB Africa MNO; Zambia/Malawi/Kenya priority pilots; Mastercard cross-border API (2023); 14 markets; 100M+ users

name: Thunes
role: Cross-border payment superhighway; CALP-engaged April 2024; treasury pre-funding in local currencies; one contract covers Haiti+Pacific+Africa corridors

name: Onafriq
role: Pan-African mobile money hub; 43 markets; 1B+ wallets; 500M bank accounts; Visa Pay DRC partnership (2025); CCHB Africa routing layer

name: Remitly
role: Digital remittance platform; connects to MonCash Haiti and Digicel Pacific; potential diaspora co-funding rail for CCHB

name: TearFund
role: UK faith-based NGO; documented parametric insurance adopter per Global Parametrics Feb 2026 whitepaper

name: MercyCorps
role: International NGO; anticipatory action and DFS/CVA delivery in fragile states

name: GiveDirectly
role: Pioneer in unconditional direct cash transfers; global benchmark for last-mile CVA delivery efficiency and cost-effectiveness

name: Jokalante
role: Senegal/Gambia; SMS/voice/USSD agricultural and financial information delivery; AA last-mile link for West Africa CCHB pilots

name: Togglecorp
role: Humanitarian data analytics firm (Kathmandu); built DEEP platform for humanitarian information management; data layer for CCHB EAP design

name: iMMAP
role: Information management NGO; geospatial data and situation analysis for humanitarian response operations

name: Craf'd
role: Design/technology consultancy in humanitarian space; UX and tech implementation for beneficiary-facing CCHB tools

name: World Bank / IBRD
role: Issued $4.5B+ cat bonds; IBRD Treasury SPV; Jamaica cat bond sponsor; convener of the sovereign parametric market globally

name: UNCDF
role: UN Capital Development Fund; DFS/mobile money financial inclusion in LDCs; active in Pacific (Vanuatu, Fiji) — CCHB last-mile DFS ally

name: UNDRR
role: UN disaster risk reduction; Sendai Framework; advocacy for DRF instruments and anticipatory action policy frameworks

name: CGAP
role: World Bank Group DFS standards body and grant-maker; sets financial inclusion norms for mobile money; funds research and pilots on digital financial services for the poor — relevant as a technical assistance funder and standard-setter for the last-mile DFS rails that CCHB payouts depend on

name: ADRiFi
role: African Disaster Risk Financing Initiative; joint AU-AfDB-UNDP programme; supports African governments to develop national disaster risk finance strategies and access sovereign parametric instruments; operationalises the link between AU member states and instruments like ARC and CCRIF — natural institutional home for CCHB sovereign corridor negotiations in Africa

name: IFRC / DREF
role: DREF anticipatory pillar; CHF 85M allocated across 167 operations (2024); EAP trigger framework; primary CCHB humanitarian sponsor candidate; bridge financing for emergency operations in Senegal; technical support for EAP protocol development with Senegalese Red Cross on flood Early Action Protocols

name: WFP
role: Parametric insurance pioneer since 2006; ARC Mozambique payout recipient ($3.6M, 2024/25); large-scale CVA delivery globally

name: UNICEF
role: Documented parametric insurance adopter; CVA and cash transfer delivery at scale for children in crisis settings

name: OCHA
role: Humanitarian coordination; CERF pooled fund ($600M/year); triggers emergency appeals that interact with cat bond payout timing

name: UNDP
role: Development programme; GCF accredited entity; led $103M EW4All early warning project (2024) with IFRC/WMO/UNDRR

name: FAO
role: Food and agriculture; 2.33B food insecure globally (2023); smallholder farmer data underpins CCHB beneficiary definition and ARC trigger design

name: IOM
role: International Organization for Migration; DTM displacement tracking matrix; beneficiary registry for conflict/disaster displacement contexts

name: CRIF
role: InsuResilience Climate Risk Insurance Facility; BMZ-funded grant mechanism that co-finances premium costs for parametric climate risk insurance in developing countries; operational blueprint for the CCHB premium subsidy layer — validates the donor-pays-premium model at scale

name: Jamaica CAT Bond
role: World Bank IBRD-issued $185M sovereign catastrophe bond for Jamaica (2021); parametric trigger for hurricane wind speed; 3-year term; first Caribbean sovereign cat bond with a humanitarian payout mechanism; canonical CCHB structural reference — same SPV-IBRD architecture, same parametric trigger logic

name: African Risk Capacity
role: AU-affiliated pan-African sovereign parametric risk pool; AU-backed, 36 member states; Senegal subscriber since 2014 — USD 30M drought cap via Africa Risk View satellite index; ARC Replica sub-sovereign window enables CSO mirror policies (Start Network, Oxfam); Oxfam 2023 Senegal payout reached 31,942 beneficiaries (54% women); Mozambique cyclone payout ($5.4M 2024/25) shows mechanism works when trigger is well-calibrated; ARC Malawi 2015–16 basis risk episode is canonical CCHB trigger calibration cautionary tale

name: ClimaCash+
role: IBISA Network's flagship product combining satellite parametric insurance with direct mobile cash transfer to smallholders on trigger; deployed in Africa and Asia; structurally the closest existing product to CCHB — same parametric trigger logic, same last-mile mobile payout rail, same target beneficiary profile; key proof-of-concept reference for CCHB investors and donors

name: DREF Anticipatory Action
role: IFRC Disaster Relief Emergency Fund anticipatory action window; pre-approved EAP triggers release funds before a disaster strikes; CHF 85M allocated across 167 operations (2024); the closest operational precedent to CCHB's automatic payout chain — same trigger-before-event logic, same humanitarian delivery infrastructure

name: Agrecol Afrique
role: Agroecology NGO based in Thiès, Senegal; promotes ecological farming transitions for smallholder farmers across West Africa; technical advisory on crop diversity and soil resilience — directly informs CCHB beneficiary vulnerability profiling in Senegal

name: ENDA Pronat
role: ENDA Graf Sahel's pesticides and natural farming programme; long-standing Senegal-based advocacy and field network promoting agroecological practices and reducing agro-chemical dependency among smallholders; strong community mobilisation capacity relevant to CCHB beneficiary registration

name: SCALA
role: Scaling up Agroecology programme (FAO/EU); supports national governments and civil society to embed agroecology in policy and finance frameworks; active in Senegal; relevant as institutional bridge between CCHB trigger design and national agricultural adaptation strategies

name: CNAAS
role: Compagnie Nationale d'Assurance Agricole du Sénégal — central actor in retail agricultural financial protection in Senegal; grew from 25,164 clients (2012) to 844,497 (2023); public-private partnership (36% state, 64% private insurers and farmer associations); state subsidises 50% of premiums plus tax exemption; covers zones Ouest (Thiès, Dakar, Louga, Diourbel), Centre-Sud and Nord; nine products: crop insurance for six cultures (groundnut, millet, sorghum, maize, cotton, rice), livestock mortality, artisanal fisheries pilot, pastoral cover; satellite and evapotranspiration index introduced as key advance; cover up to USD 10.5M per department; gap: no products yet for urban flooding or coastal erosion

name: ENSA Thiès
role: École Nationale Supérieure d'Agriculture de Thiès — Senegal's principal public agricultural university; agronomic research, soil and crop science, training of agricultural extension agents; potential CCHB partner for trigger index ground-truthing, basis-risk validation, and local academic credibility in Senegal

name: Africa Re
role: African Reinsurance Corporation — pan-African multilateral reinsurer owned by 42 African states and the AfDB; largest reinsurer on the continent; key capacity provider for parametric and agricultural risk in Africa; co-author (with Gallagher Re) of scalable risk transfer framework directly relevant to CCHB structuring

name: Gallagher Re
role: Global reinsurance broker and risk advisory; co-author (with Africa Re) of 'Accelerating action through scalable risk transfer schemes'; expertise in parametric product structuring and ILS placement; natural CCHB deal-structuring and placement partner

name: Dalili Advisors
role: Boutique advisory firm specialising in climate risk finance and insurance structuring in African markets; bridges international ILS capital and local African risk carriers; relevant as in-market structuring and placement advisor for CCHB in Sub-Saharan contexts

name: IBLI & Animal Encroachment Insurance
role: Index-Based Livestock Insurance (IBLI), developed by ILRI — satellite-derived NDVI trigger for drought-related livestock mortality; operational in Kenya and Ethiopia; canonical precedent for CCHB index design for pastoralist beneficiaries. Animal Encroachment Insurance covers crop damage from wildlife incursion using parametric triggers — extends the index logic to human-wildlife conflict perils relevant to CCHB trigger design in sub-Saharan contexts

name: Accelerating Action through Scalable Risk Transfer Schemes
role: Joint Gallagher Re & Africa Re framework paper; articulates a structured pathway for scaling parametric risk transfer across African markets through layered risk pools, donor premium subsidy, and local carrier capacity-building; direct conceptual reference for CCHB financial architecture and donor engagement strategy

name: Allianz Re
role: Reinsurance arm of Allianz Group; active in parametric and agricultural reinsurance in emerging markets; treaty capacity provider for index-based products in Africa and Asia; relevant CCHB reinsurance layer partner alongside Hannover Re and Africa Re

name: One Acre Fund
role: Delivers inclusive farmer-first insurance via One Acre Shield across Africa; 5M+ direct clients; designs and scales climate and household products grounded in farmer data and research; OAF Re is their mission-aligned reinsurance facility reducing intermediation costs and accelerating payouts; collaborates with insurers and aggregators to extend protection across the broader market; primary reference for CCHB beneficiary cost modelling (USD 60/person/yr Climate Smart Agri default) and last-mile delivery infrastructure

name: DRIVE
role: De-Risking, Inclusion and Value Enhancement project — GIZ/BMZ initiative supporting smallholder agricultural insurance markets in Africa; works with local insurers, governments, and reinsurers to de-risk index-based product launches; operational blueprint for CCHB premium subsidy and local carrier capacity-building approach

name: ADAPTA Technology
role: AI-enabled agricultural risk management platform for banks; integrates climate, water, soil, and ecosystem data to evaluate agricultural loan risk; built on AWS Bedrock and RAG framework; seamless API or cloud dashboard integration with existing credit workflows; also enables carbon farming and ecosystem-linked finance innovation; operations in Africa and Latin America — natural CCHB partner for trigger index data infrastructure and beneficiary credit-risk profiling

name: ACRE Africa
role: Agriculture and Climate Risk Enterprise — risk management solutions designer linking stakeholders to localised parametric insurance and climate adaptation strategies; leverages AI analytics, digital platforms, and last-mile networks; 4M+ farmers insured across 19+ African countries; data-driven solutions that unlock credit, strengthen resilience, and power agricultural transformation in Africa; major parametric insurer ecosystem actor alongside Pula and IBISA

name: ANACIM
role: Agence Nationale de l'Aviation Civile et de la Météorologie — Senegal's national meteorological agency; produces seasonal and 10-day forecasts; operates automated weather station network; primary data source for anticipatory action trigger protocols in Senegal; key CCHB trigger data infrastructure partner

name: Ministry of Interior Senegal (ORSEC)
role: Senegal's national emergency coordination body (Organisation des Secours); activates and coordinates civil protection resources across government in disaster events; institutional anchor for CCHB payout activation chain at sovereign level in Senegal

name: Senegalese Red Cross (SRCS)
role: National Red Cross Society; first response and community-level volunteer assessment and distribution; partner in ORSEC emergency coordination; piloting Early Action Protocol for floods with IFRC/DREF support; natural CCHB last-mile humanitarian delivery partner in Senegal

name: CNCAS
role: Caisse Nationale de Crédit Agricole du Sénégal — national agricultural credit fund; provides seasonal production credit to smallholder farmers; distributes insurance products bundled with agricultural inputs; key CCHB bundling and financing partner bridging credit access and parametric coverage in Senegal

name: R4 Rural Resilience Initiative
role: WFP/Oxfam/IRI/CNAAS integrated resilience programme combining index drought insurance (payable in labour for the poorest), Savings for Change clubs, credit access, and natural resource management; women are primary beneficiaries with explicit gender equality in local management committees; operational in Senegal's Fatick, Kaolack and Kaffrine regions; model considered replicable — direct structural precedent for CCHB's multi-layer risk finance architecture

name: Farmer Cooperatives Senegal (GIEs / Fédérations)
role: Senegalese farmer cooperatives and economic interest groups (GIEs) acting as insurance enrolment aggregators; distribute index products; manage collective storage; procure inputs collectively; negotiate with SONACOS and exporters post-shock; essential last-mile distribution and beneficiary registration infrastructure for CCHB in Senegal

name: PlaNet Guarantee
role: Insurtech and insurance broker specialising in parametric product design and distribution for pastoralists and crop farmers in Africa; works with private insurers and reinsurers to structure and place index-based products; active in Francophone West Africa including Senegal; relevant CCHB product design and distribution partner

name: Oxfam / CARE
role: International NGOs providing technical assistance for R4 Rural Resilience Initiative; food security monitoring; community engagement and beneficiary support in climate shock response; field implementation partners in Senegal complementing FAO agronomic support

name: Community First Responders Senegal
role: Youth associations, religious leaders (imams), and community solidarity networks (tontines, dahiras) — the actual first responders before any formal system activates in Senegal; informal risk-sharing infrastructure; critical CCHB last-mile trust and legitimacy layer for payout acceptance and distribution

name: Nephila Capital
role: One of the world's largest dedicated ILS fund managers (Markel subsidiary); manages cat bond and ILS strategies across multiple funds; proven appetite for parametric humanitarian structures — primary institutional ILS capital target for CCHB bond tranche placement

name: Fermat Capital
role: Specialist ILS and cat bond investment manager; one of the oldest and largest dedicated cat bond funds; manages multi-billion USD ILS portfolios with deep parametric instrument expertise; key CCHB bond investor target alongside Nephila

name: TerraPay
role: Cross-border payment infrastructure provider connecting 3.7B+ mobile wallets, bank accounts, and cards across 120+ countries; enables instant cross-border mobile money transfers; relevant CCHB settlement rail for international premium flows and payout transfers to MNO wallets in recipient countries

name: Wave
role: Mobile money operator with near-zero fee model; dominant in Senegal and Côte d'Ivoire; 2M+ active users in Senegal; low-cost last-mile digital wallet infrastructure; strong agent network; natural CCHB payout rail for Senegal corridor given fee structure and reach

name: CPMI — CARD Pioneer Microinsurance
role: CARD Pioneer Microinsurance Inc. — Philippines-based cooperative microinsurer; one of Asia's largest microinsurance providers serving low-income households; disaster and agricultural parametric products; operational model for CCHB's cooperative distribution and low-ticket coverage approach in Asia-Pacific corridors

name: Guy Carpenter / Marsh Re
role: Reinsurance broking arm of Marsh McLennan; one of the two largest global reinsurance brokers; structuring and placement of cat bonds, ILS, and parametric reinsurance treaties; natural CCHB lead placement broker for fiat tranche and reinsurance layer alongside Gallagher Re

name: PT Reasuransi Maipark
role: Indonesian state-backed specialist reinsurer for earthquake and catastrophe risk; key capacity provider for parametric natural disaster products in Southeast Asia; operational model for nationally anchored parametric reinsurance in a developing-country context — relevant for CCHB Pacific/Asia corridor structuring

name: MiCRO (Howden)
role: Microinsurance Catastrophe Risk Organisation — specialist parametric microinsurer, now within Howden Group; designs and underwrites catastrophe risk products for low-income populations in Central America and beyond; donor-backed capital structure with parametric trigger design for smallholder and community beneficiaries; direct structural analog for CCHB humanitarian parametric layer

name: MicroEnsure
role: Pioneer microinsurance intermediary (now Bima); designs and distributes parametric and life microinsurance products via MNO partnerships across Africa and Asia; reached 30M+ low-income clients; demonstrated MNO-bundled insurance distribution model directly relevant to CCHB last-mile delivery architecture

name: Sanasa Insurance
role: Sri Lankan cooperative insurer owned by the Sanasa Development Bank network; provides agricultural and disaster insurance to cooperative members and smallholder farmers; cooperative ownership model and parametric product suite make it a relevant CCHB in-country risk carrier reference for Asia-Pacific corridors

name: Green Delta Insurance Company
role: Bangladesh's largest private insurer; pioneered agricultural index insurance and weather-parametric products for smallholder farmers and aquaculture; key in-country underwriting partner for CCHB Bangladesh corridor; demonstrated that commercially viable parametric agricultural insurance is achievable in a climate-vulnerable LDC context

name: FSN — Fonds de Solidarité Nationale
role: Senegal's national emergency household response fund (created 2021); relies on the RNU social registry, mobile money transfers (Orange, Free, Expresso), and DGPSN social operator network; specific mechanisms: floods (FCFA 3B for 20,000 households), fires (FCFA 200M for 1,000 households), food insecurity (FCFA 3.3B for 27,537 households); diagnosed gaps: under-funded relative to need, transfers too slow, coverage focused on drought/food insecurity — leaving urban flooding and coastal erosion unaddressed; direct CCHB complementarity for rapid parametric payout filling FSN response gaps

name: PAPSA — Projet d'Appui à la Protection Sociale Adaptative
role: World Bank / Government of Senegal adaptive social protection project; most systemic intervention in the portfolio — targets 130,000 beneficiaries with economic inclusion programmes and 51,000 households affected by climate shocks; simultaneously strengthens RNU (target 1M households by 2025), mobile payment systems, and grievance mechanisms; 2022 flood response demonstrated operational effectiveness in real conditions; directly validates the CCHB assumption that existing social protection infrastructure can serve as parametric payout distribution rails in Senegal

name: ARC Replica
role: Sub-sovereign window of African Risk Capacity allowing CSOs and NGOs to subscribe mirror parametric policies covering populations unreachable by the sovereign mechanism; Start Network is ARC Replica Partner since 2018 — first non-governmental policyholder in ARC history — purchasing drought policies in Senegal, Somalia and Zimbabwe; 2019 Senegal trigger: six Start Network member agencies (Action Against Hunger, CRS, Oxfam, Plan International, Save the Children, World Vision) distributed enriched flour and cash to 335,000+ people across seven regions; Oxfam 2023 Senegal payout reached 31,942 beneficiaries (54% women); most complete documented end-to-end parametric humanitarian payout chain yet deployed — canonical operational reference for CCHB's dual-rail sovereign/humanitarian architecture

name: FAGI — Fondation pour l'Adaptation de la Genève Internationale
role: Foundation co-created in 2025 by the State of Geneva and the Hans Wilsdorf Foundation (Rolex); dedicated to climate adaptation through the leverage of Geneva's international organisations, expertise and finance networks; sits at the intersection of Geneva's humanitarian and financial ecosystem — natural convener and co-financier for CCHB given its Geneva IHL/IHO institutional roots and the Hans Wilsdorf Foundation's philanthropic capital

name: SME Finance Forum — Virtual Marketplace
role: IFC-managed global network of 300+ member financial institutions across 190+ countries; established 2012 by the G20; monthly Virtual Marketplace pitch events connect insurtechs and fintechs to banks, MFIs, and DFIs — 150+ companies presented, 2,500+ participants since 2021; dedicated insurtech-for-smallholder-farmers edition directly showcased PULA, ACRE Africa, One Acre Fund and analogous players; key discovery and partnership channel for CCHB to reach FSP distribution partners and impact investors at scale

name: FONGIT
role: Fondation Genevoise pour l'Innovation Technologique; Switzerland's largest tech incubator (est. 1991); state-backed non-profit supporting 250+ deep-tech startups in ICT, advanced engineering and life sciences; provides coaching, office space, governance and financing guidance; embedded in Geneva's innovation ecosystem alongside humanitarian and international finance institutions — natural bridge between parametric insurtech startups and the Geneva IHL/IHO cluster

name: Cardano Foundation
role: Swiss-based not-for-profit (Zug) that standardises, protects and promotes the Cardano blockchain protocol; focuses on payments, digital identity, real-world asset tokenisation and DeFi adoption; stewards open permissionless public infrastructure used for smart-contract-based parametric triggers, on-chain settlement and decentralised insurance primitives

name: Reimagining Fundraising
role: INGO open-innovation collaborative (est. 2020) that crowdsources fundraising solutions for leading international nonprofits; issues open calls to external innovators, evaluates submissions and supports pilots — relevant as a channel to surface and scale novel parametric / blended-finance fundraising instruments across the humanitarian sector

name: HIF Hub — Danish Red Cross
role: Danish Red Cross initiative that helps humanitarian practitioners structure projects to attract investment and financing; provides guidance, tools and partnerships to develop investible humanitarian instruments and build organisational capacity for innovative finance — direct bridge between field implementers and blended/impact finance actors
tags: Danish Red Cross|humanitarian finance|blended finance|capacity building|investible projects

name: TRC–IFRC Islamic Development Bank WASH Programme
role: First Red Cross National Society in Africa (Togolese Red Cross) to serve as implementing partner in a sovereign loan-financed WASH programme; Islamic Development Bank sovereign lending channelled through the government unlocked substantial sustainable WASH investment; demonstrates how National Societies can access development finance via IFRC partnership — replicable model for government-led development finance in CCHB target corridors
tags: Togolese Red Cross|IFRC|Islamic Development Bank|sovereign loan|WASH

name: Malawi Red Cross Parametric Flood Insurance
role: Parametric flood insurance product developed by Malawi Red Cross, Danish Red Cross, ARC and JBA Risk Management with InsuResilience Solutions Fund support; customised flood model uses rainfall and impact-based forecasts to trigger layered funding — early action, emergency response, and recovery; integrates community-level hazard mapping and mobile technology for rapid coordination; demonstrates an end-to-end parametric humanitarian payout architecture directly analogous to CCHB
tags: Malawi Red Cross|Danish Red Cross|ARC|parametric|flood insurance

name: ICRC Goma West Water Blended Finance
role: ICRC committed CHF 8M as initial grant capital to leverage USD 42M from the World Bank for sustainable water infrastructure in eastern DRC — a 9× multiplier replacing decades of emergency water trucking; now serves 2.5M people with a pipeline expansion projected to reach 11M across Goma, Bukavu and Bunia; model being replicated in Uganda, Ghana and South Sudan — canonical humanitarian blended finance precedent for CCHB first-loss / DFI layering architecture

name: IFRC-DREF Risk Transfer Insurance
role: Insurance mechanism layered on top of IFRC's Disaster Relief Emergency Fund (est. 1979); donors contribute insurance premiums to transfer peak-year risk to private reinsurance markets when natural hazard disaster funding requests exceed available DREF resources; partners: IFRC, Aon, Centre for Disaster Protection; operationally validates the donor-pays-premium / risk-transfer model central to CCHB — proof that parametric risk transfer can sustain a major humanitarian fund against climate-driven demand spikes

name: Systematic Observations Financing Facility (SOFF)
role: WMO-hosted UN facility (est. 2022) that finances the expansion and modernisation of weather, climate and hydrological observation networks in LDCs and SIDS — the upstream data infrastructure on which parametric triggers depend; provides grants and technical assistance to strengthen NMHSs so they can generate the station-level data required for index-based parametric products; closing the observation gap in CCHB target corridors is a prerequisite for robust trigger design and index verification

name: IADB (Inter-American Development Bank)
role: Multilateral development bank for Latin America and the Caribbean; finances climate resilience, disaster risk finance and social protection programmes across CCHB-relevant Caribbean and Central American corridors; houses the DRFIP (Disaster Risk Finance and Insurance Program) and has co-structured parametric sovereign risk transfer transactions for Caribbean and Central American governments — natural DFI partner for CCHB first-loss tranches and premium subsidy structures in the LAC region

name: Stellar Development Foundation
role: Non-profit that develops and maintains the Stellar blockchain network — an open, low-cost, high-throughput Layer-1 optimised for payments and asset tokenisation; powers the Stellar Aid Assist programme deployed with UNHCR for cash-based assistance in Ukraine and Afghanistan; Stellar's USDC on-chain settlement and wallet infrastructure is directly analogous to the CCHB token rail — fast, compliant digital cash delivery to displaced and vulnerable populations

name: UNHCR (UN Refugee Agency)
role: UN Refugee Agency providing protection and assistance to 117M+ forcibly displaced people globally; partnered with Stellar Development Foundation on Aid Assist — on-chain USDC cash transfers to refugees in Ukraine and Afghanistan; manages large-scale cash-based intervention programmes; key field implementer and distribution partner for CCHB-type trigger-to-wallet payout chains in displacement contexts

name: UNHCR Innovation Fund
role: UNHCR's dedicated fund for testing and scaling innovative solutions in refugee contexts; provides seed grants and support for pilots across digital identity, fintech, blockchain and unconventional financing models; has funded blockchain-based cash assistance, digital ID and staking experiments — direct funder of proof-of-concept work directly analogous to CCHB's token rail and trigger-to-wallet architecture

name: UNHCR — Cardano Staking for Refugees
role: UNHCR Switzerland and Cardano Foundation partnership enabling ADA holders to delegate staking rewards directly to UNHCR — first use of proof-of-stake blockchain mechanics for humanitarian fundraising at scale; demonstrates that public blockchain infrastructure (Cardano) can channel value to a major humanitarian organisation with full legal and compliance sign-off; establishes the Cardano-UNHCR relationship that underpins CCHB's Cardano-rail credibility and opens the path for on-chain parametric payout pilots

name: The Giving Block
role: Leading crypto fundraising platform for nonprofits and charities; processes Bitcoin and multi-cryptocurrency donations for 2,000+ vetted organisations including UN agencies and major INGOs; provides the donor-side infrastructure for crypto-native philanthropy — the missing link between crypto wealth and humanitarian funding flows; relevant to CCHB as the mechanism through which ILS/DeFi investors and crypto donors can direct capital toward premium subsidies and proof-of-concept grants

name: Bitcoin Humanitarian Alliance
role: Coalition of Bitcoin-focused organisations and humanitarian actors working to apply Bitcoin and Lightning Network infrastructure to humanitarian response — cash-based assistance, remittance corridors, and inflation-resistant savings for displaced populations; convenes practitioners exploring non-custodial, censorship-resistant payment rails in conflict and fragile-state contexts relevant to CCHB last-mile delivery

name: Mercy Corps Ventures
role: Investment and innovation arm of Mercy Corps that backs and tests fintech and blockchain solutions for frontier markets; runs pilots combining parametric insurance triggers with on-chain smart contracts and stablecoin payment rails; published evidence from Kenya and Syria pilots showing 60–96% cost and time reductions vs. traditional humanitarian payment channels — the most rigorous field evidence base for CCHB's dual-rail value proposition

name: Mercy Corps Ventures — Kenya Parametric Pasture Pilot
role: Mercy Corps Ventures Kenya pilot used a smart contract holding donated funds in escrow programmed to disburse only if satellite-verified pasture conditions were distressful; reached 262 pastoralists at 2.5% end-to-end transaction cost vs. 10–35% via traditional methods — a 75% cost reduction; satellite trigger → smart-contract escrow → direct payout chain is structurally identical to the CCHB parametric-oracle → token-rail → wallet architecture

name: Mercy Corps Ventures — Syria HesabPay Stablecoin Pilot
role: Mercy Corps Ventures Syria pilot using stablecoin payment rails (HesabPay) achieved a 96% drop in payment time (from 28 days to under 1 day) and 60% cost reduction; 72% of participants preferred stablecoin payments over cash for future programming; demonstrates that stablecoin rails are accepted and preferred by conflict-affected populations — direct validation of the CCHB token rail's viability for humanitarian cash transfer in fragile contexts

name: Encointer — Tanzania & Nigeria Community Currency
role: Encointer Foundation deployed local community currencies on a Substrate-based blockchain in Tanzania and Nigeria — regular proof-of-personhood ceremonies establish digital identity and distribute a basic income token to verified participants; demonstrates that permissionless blockchain infrastructure can deliver recurring digital cash to unbanked rural populations without bank accounts or smartphones — a precedent for CCHB's KYC-lite token-rail payout to beneficiaries in Sub-Saharan Africa

name: Global Shield against Climate Risks
role: G7 and V20 initiative launched at COP27 (2022) to close the protection gap for climate-vulnerable countries; mobilises grants and concessional finance for pre-arranged disaster risk finance instruments including parametric insurance and anticipatory action mechanisms; primary vehicle for scaling sovereign and sub-sovereign parametric coverage in LDCs and SIDS — directly relevant as a premium co-funder and blended capital mobiliser for CCHB pilot corridors

name: Europa Re
role: Southeast European regional reinsurance company; provides catastrophe reinsurance capacity and parametric risk transfer solutions for natural disasters (earthquake, flood) across the Balkans, Caucasus and Central Asia; develops national and regional disaster risk finance frameworks and sovereign parametric programmes in emerging markets underserved by global reinsurers — relevant CCHB reinsurance capacity partner for Eastern European and Central Asian corridors

name: Stellar Aid Assist — Ukraine & Afghanistan
role: UNHCR and Stellar Development Foundation deployed blockchain-based cash assistance to Ukrainian refugees (Poland) and Afghan beneficiaries: USDC distributed directly to smartphone wallets on the Stellar network; recipients convert to local currency via MoneyGram cash-out; no bank account required; transaction costs under $0.01; 2022–2024 pilots reached thousands of households — the most direct deployed precedent for the CCHB token rail (trigger → on-chain stablecoin → wallet → cash-out agent)

name: Save the Waves — Surf Ecosystem Insurance
role: World's first parametric insurance product protecting surf ecosystems and coastal economies from climate-driven extreme weather; piloted in El Salvador (Oriente Salvaje World Surfing Reserve) with support from ORRAA and the Government of Canada; predetermined payouts triggered by exceedance of environmental thresholds (wind speed, precipitation) release rapid funding for ecosystem recovery and community income loss — demonstrates parametric trigger design for non-sovereign natural capital and nature-based economy protection, replicable across Save the Waves' 100+ World Surfing Reserves network targeting 1,000 ecosystems by 2030

name: ORRAA (Ocean Risk and Resilience Action Alliance)
role: Multi-sector convener connecting finance, insurance, governments, multilateral organisations, civil society and local partners to pioneer ocean risk finance and insurance products; mission to activate USD 500M of investment by 2030 to build resilience of 250M climate-vulnerable coastal people in the Global South; develops blue finance instruments including parametric products for coral reefs, mangroves, and surf ecosystems — co-structured the Save the Waves El Salvador surf insurance pilot; relevant convener for CCHB coastal and SIDS corridor design

name: Environment and Climate Change Canada (ECCC)
role: Canadian federal department responsible for climate change policy and international climate finance; co-funded the ORRAA/Save the Waves parametric surf ecosystem insurance pilot in El Salvador; supports the Caribbean Catastrophe Risk Insurance Facility (CCRIF) and broader disaster risk finance instruments in vulnerable countries; channels Canada's USD 5.3B climate finance commitment through bilateral and multilateral mechanisms — relevant premium co-funder and bilateral donor for CCHB pilots in Caribbean and Pacific SIDS corridors

name: MIT Solve
role: MIT's open innovation marketplace that connects social entrepreneurs with funding, resources and a global community; runs annual Challenges including a Climate & Environment track and a Financial Inclusion track — both directly relevant to CCHB; Solve awards equity-free funding and provides acceleration support; has surfaced and funded early-stage parametric and DeFi insurance ventures; relevant as a discovery channel and proof-of-concept grant source for CCHB startups and insurtech partners

name: GSMA
role: Mobile industry association representing 750+ operators in 220+ countries; funds the GSMA Mobile for Humanitarian Innovation (M4H) programme with grants and technical assistance for mobile-based humanitarian cash transfer pilots; sets interoperability standards for mobile money and runs the Mobile Money API programme — underpins the last-mile MNO rails central to CCHB payout architecture; relevant as a standards-setter, grant channel and MNO convener

name: ICMIF (International Cooperative and Mutual Insurance Federation)
role: Global federation of 200+ cooperative and mutual insurance organisations in 60+ countries; advocates for inclusive insurance and mutual risk-pooling models; runs the Microinsurance Network partnership and supports member development of parametric and index-based products for smallholder and low-income markets — natural CCHB convener for accessing mutual insurer capacity and community-owned risk-pooling structures in target corridors

name: Microinsurance Network
role: Multi-stakeholder platform of 300+ members (insurers, donors, development agencies, governments) dedicated to expanding access to insurance for low-income populations; produces market intelligence, facilitates knowledge exchange, and co-develops standards for inclusive insurance; has published key reference work on parametric microinsurance design and basis risk — primary knowledge and convening hub for the microinsurance layer of the CCHB architecture

name: IBISA–Ensuro–One Acre Fund Kenya Parametric Pilot
role: Blockchain-based parametric crop insurance deployed in Kenya: IBISA designed the satellite-based trigger index (NDVI); Ensuro provided the on-chain capital pool and smart-contract payout engine; One Acre Fund distributed the product to smallholder farmers and handled last-mile enrolment; when the satellite index crossed the drought threshold the smart contract automatically disbursed USDC to policyholders — no claims adjuster required; first live deployment combining parametric trigger data, DeFi capital provision and humanitarian last-mile distribution in a single stack directly analogous to CCHB
